visuals.title: Introduction to Data Link Layer
visuals.description: Overview of the Data Link Layer's role in the OSI model, showing its position between the Physical and Network Layers, its services including framing, error detection and correction, flow control, and access control.

diagrams.title: OSI Model - Data Link Layer Position
diagrams.description: A layered diagram showing all seven OSI layers with the Data Link Layer highlighted between Physical Layer (Layer 1) and Network Layer (Layer 3). Arrows indicate data flow direction.
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand where the Data Link Layer sits in the OSI stack and its relationship with adjacent layers for node-to-node communication.

diagrams.title: Data Link Layer Sub-layers
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the two sub-layers of the Data Link Layer: Logical Link Control (LLC) and Media Access Control (MAC), with their respective functions listed.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the conceptual division of the Data Link Layer and helps students understand the distinction between LLC (upper sublayer) and MAC (lower sublayer) responsibilities.

diagrams.title: Types of Transmission Errors
diagrams.description: A comparative visualization showing Single-Bit Error versus Burst Error in a data stream, with the corrupted bits highlighted.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the difference between single-bit errors (one bit flipped) and burst errors (multiple consecutive bits corrupted), helping students understand why different detection/correction methods are needed.

diagrams.title: Frame Structure
diagrams.description: A diagram of a typical data frame showing the header and trailer components with their fields: Frame Start Flag, Source MAC Address, Destination MAC Address, Control Information, Data Payload, Error Detection Code, and Frame End Flag.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how the Data Link Layer encapsulates network layer data into frames, showing the structure of header, data, and trailer sections with error detection codes.

diagrams.title: Error Detection and Correction Process
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the error handling process from sender to receiver, including encoding with error detection bits, transmission, decoding, error detection, and correction or retransmission request.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the complete error control process showing how errors are detected at the receiver and the actions taken (either correction or retransmission request) to ensure reliable data delivery.
